    <h1 {...{
      "id": "clipboard-operations"
    }}>{`Clipboard Operations`}</h1>
    <p>{`Integrate your plugin with the system clipboard to read and write text data`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP provides clipboard APIs that let your plugin `}<strong parentName="p">{`read from`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`write to`}</strong>{` the system clipboard—enabling users to copy, paste, and share content between your plugin and other applications. This guide covers how to use the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Global%20Members/Data%20Transfers/Clipboard.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`Clipboard`}</inlineCode>{` API`}</a>{`, declare the necessary permissions, and handle common use cases.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "system-requirements"
    }}>{`System requirements`}</h2>
    <p>{`Please make make sure your development environment uses the following `}<strong parentName="p">{`minimum versions`}</strong>{` to avoid compatibility issues:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest v5`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "clipboard-security"
    }}>{`Clipboard Security`}</h2>
    <p>{`By default, UXP plugins can't access the system clipboard; this protects users from unwanted data access. If your plugin needs to read or write clipboard content, `}<strong parentName="p">{`you must declare`}</strong>{` the right `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
      }}>{`clipboard permission`}</a>{` in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`Choose the most appropriate permission level:`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Permission`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Access Level`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Use When`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`"read"`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Read-only`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Your plugin only needs to paste or read data`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`"readAndWrite"`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Read and write access`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Your plugin needs to copy and paste data`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Pick the least-permissive option that meets your needs`}</p>
    <p>{`In future versions, users may be asked to grant consent for clipboard access, and they'll be more comfortable approving read-only access unless your plugin clearly needs to write data.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "using-the-clipboard-api"
    }}>{`Using the Clipboard API`}</h2>
    <p>{`The clipboard is accessed through `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`navigator.clipboard`}</inlineCode>{`, which provides two main methods:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`setContent()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Write data to the clipboard.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`getContent()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Read data from the clipboard.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Both methods work with MIME type objects, where keys represent data formats (like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"text/plain"`}</inlineCode>{`) and values contain the actual content.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-writing-to-the-clipboard"
    }}>{`Example: Writing to the Clipboard`}</h3>
    <p>{`Here's how to copy text to the system clipboard.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Copy formatted text to the clipboard ✂️
async function copyToClipboard(text) {
  try {
    await navigator.clipboard.setContent({
      "text/plain": text
    });
    console.log("✅ Text copied to clipboard");

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("❌ Failed to copy to clipboard:", err);
  }
}

// Example usage
copyToClipboard("Welcome to UXP for Premiere!");
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "clipboard": "readAndWrite"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-reading-from-the-clipboard"
    }}>{`Example: Reading from the Clipboard`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can also read content that users have copied from other applications.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Paste text from the clipboard 📋
async function pasteFromClipboard() {
  try {
    const clipboardData = await navigator.clipboard.getContent();

    if (clipboardData["text/plain"]) {
      console.log(\`Pasted text: \${clipboardData["text/plain"]}\`);
      return clipboardData["text/plain"];
    } else {
      console.log("⚠️ No text data found on clipboard");
      return null;
    }

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("❌ Failed to read from clipboard:", err);
  }
}

// Example usage
pasteFromClipboard();
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "manifestjson-1"
    }}>{`manifest.json`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "clipboard": "read"
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Notice how this example uses `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"read"`}</inlineCode>{` permission since it only needs to `}<strong parentName="p">{`read`}</strong>{` from the clipboard, not write to it. Always choose the minimum permission level your plugin requires.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-copy-and-paste-together"
    }}>{`Example: Copy and Paste Together`}</h3>
    <p>{`In many workflows, you'll want to both read and write clipboard data—for example, transforming text or syncing data between your plugin and external tools.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Transform clipboard text to uppercase
async function transformClipboardText() {
  try {
    // Read current clipboard content
    const data = await navigator.clipboard.getContent();

    if (data["text/plain"]) {
      const originalText = data["text/plain"];
      const transformedText = originalText.toUpperCase();

      // Write the transformed text back
      await navigator.clipboard.setContent({
        "text/plain": transformedText
      });

      console.log(\`✅ Transformed: "\${originalText}" → "\${transformedText}"\`);
    } else {
      console.log("⚠️ No text found on clipboard");
    }

  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed to transform clipboard text:", err);
  }
}

// Example usage
transformClipboardText();
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`For this use case, your manifest would need `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"readAndWrite"`}</inlineCode>{` permission.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "reference-material"
    }}>{`Reference Material`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{

      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#permissionsdefinition"
        }}>{`Manifest Permissions`}</a>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Clipboard Security`}</strong>{`: By default, plugins cannot access the system clipboard. You must explicitly declare clipboard permissions in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"read"`}</inlineCode>{` for read-only access (pasting content).`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"readAndWrite"`}</inlineCode>{` when you need to both copy and paste.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Choose the least-permissive option to avoid installation friction.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Using the API`}</strong>{`: Access the clipboard via `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`navigator.clipboard`}</inlineCode>{`:`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`setContent()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Writes data to the clipboard using MIME type objects (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`{ "text/plain": "text" }`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`getContent()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Reads data from the clipboard, returning an object keyed by MIME types.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Always wrap clipboard operations in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`try...catch`}</inlineCode>{` blocks and use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`await`}</inlineCode>{` for proper error handling.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
